FIRST check docs/learned/index.md for existing documentation on this topic. Read relevant docs before exploring raw files. Then explore codebase as necessary.
I need to understand the TUI codebase structure for adding a "Runs" tab. Explore and provide the content of these specific files:
1. `src/erk/tui/views/types.py` - ViewMode enum and VIEW_CONFIGS 2. `src/erk/tui/data/types.py` - PlanRowData dataclass 3. `src/erk/tui/data/provider_abc.py` - PlanDataProvider ABC 4. `src/erk/tui/data/real_provider.py` - RealPlanDataProvider (focus on fetch_plans method structure) 5. `src/erk/tui/data/fake_provider.py` - FakePlanDataProvider 6. `packages/erk-shared/src/erk_shared/gateway/plan_data_provider/fake.py` - shared fake provider 7. `src/erk/tui/widgets/plan_table.py` - PlanDataTable widget (to use as pattern for RunDataTable) 8. `src/erk/tui/app.py` - ErkDashApp (focus on _switch_view, _load_data, compose, on_mount methods and general structure) 9. `src/erk/tui/screens/help_screen.py` - HelpScreen 10. `src/erk/cli/commands/run/list_cmd.py` - existing _list_runs() to port logic from 11. `src/erk/cli/commands/run/shared.py` - extract_pr_number, extract_plan_number 12. `src/erk/core/display_utils.py` - format_workflow_run_id, format_workflow_outcome, format_submission_time 13. `src/erk/cli/constants.py` - WORKFLOW_COMMAND_MAP
For each file, provide the FULL content. I need to understand every detail to implement the new Runs tab correctly.

1→<!-- AUTO-GENERATED FILE - DO NOT EDIT DIRECTLY --> 2→<!-- Edit source frontmatter, then run 'erk docs sync' to regenerate. --> 3→ 4→# Agent Documentation 5→ 6→Before starting work, scan the read-when conditions below. 7→If your current task matches, read the linked document **before writing code**. 8→ 9→## Categories 10→ 11→- [architecture/](architecture/) — Explore when working on core patterns (dry-run, gateways, subprocess, shell integration). Add docs here for cross-cutting technical patterns. 12→- [capabilities/](capabilities/) 13→- [changelog/](changelog/) 14→- [checklists/](checklists/) 15→- [ci/](ci/) 16→- [claude-code/](claude-code/) 17→- [cli/](cli/) — Explore when building CLI commands or output formatting. Add docs here for Click patterns and terminal UX. 18→- [commands/](commands/) — Explore when creating or optimizing slash commands. Add docs here for command authoring patterns. 19→- [config/](config/) 20→- [configuration/](configuration/) 21→- [documentation/](documentation/) — Explore when structuring or writing documentation. Add docs here for documentation methodology and best practices. 22→- [erk/](erk/) — Explore when working with erk-specific workflows (worktrees, PR sync, Graphite). Add docs here for erk user-facing features. 23→- [erk-dev/](erk-dev/) 24→- [gateway/](gateway/) 25→- [hooks/](hooks/) — Explore when creating or debugging hooks. Add docs here for hook development patterns. 26→- [integrations/](integrations/) 27→- [objectives/](objectives/) 28→- [planning/](planning/) — Explore when working with plans, .erk/impl-context/ folders, or agent delegation. Add docs here for planning workflow patterns. 29→- [pr-operations/](pr-operations/) 30→- [refactoring/](refactoring/)
... [22 lines omitted] ...

1→--- 2→title: Tui Tripwires 3→read_when: 4→ - "working on tui code" 5→--- 6→ 7→<!-- AUTO-GENERATED FILE - DO NOT EDIT DIRECTLY --> 8→<!-- Edit source frontmatter, then run 'erk docs sync' to regenerate. --> 9→<!-- Generated from tui/*.md frontmatter --> 10→ 11→# Tui Tripwires 12→ 13→Rules triggered by matching actions in code. 14→ 15→**accessing \_status_bar without null guard** → Read [TUI Streaming Output Patterns](streaming-output.md) first. Guard \_status_bar access with `if self._status_bar is not None:` — timing issue during widget lifecycle can cause AttributeError. 16→ 17→**accessing optional gateway fields without null checks in TUI event handlers** → Read [Modal Widget Embedding Pattern](modal-widget-embedding.md) first. Gateway fields like plan_body or objective_content may be None. Always check before accessing in event handlers. 18→ 19→**adding CI check formatting to TUI screens** → Read [TUI Architecture Overview](architecture.md) first. Use src/erk/tui/formatting/ci_checks.py shared module (format_check_line, format_summary_blockquote, format_check_runs) instead of duplicating formatting logic. 20→ 21→**adding a CLI flag that affects behavior without checking TUI command palette** → Read [TUI Command Registration](tui-command-registration.md) first. TUI command palette generates shell commands via src/erk/tui/commands/registry.py. When adding CLI flags that change behavior, check if TUI-generated commands need the flag too. 22→ 23→**adding a DataTable column with add_column(key=...)** → Read [TUI Architecture Overview](architecture.md) first. Column key is a data binding contract — must match data field name. Silent failure when mismatched. 24→ 25→**adding a column to PlanDataTable without updating make_plan_row** → Read [Column Addition Pattern](column-addition-pattern.md) first. Column additions require 5 coordinated changes. See column-addition-pattern.md for the complete checklist. 26→ 27→**adding a command without an availability predicate** → Read [TUI Command Architecture](action-inventory.md) first. Every command needs an is_available predicate based on PlanRowData field presence. Commands without predicates appear when they can't execute. 28→ 29→**adding a field to PlanRowData without updating make_plan_row** → Read [TUI Data Contract](data-contract.md) first. The fake's make_plan_row() helper must stay in sync. Add the new field with a sensible default there too, or all TUI tests will break. 30→
... [111 lines omitted] ...
35→**adding a new ViewMode without updating VIEW_CONFIGS** → Read [TUI View Switching](view-switching.md) first. Every ViewMode must have a corresponding ViewConfig in VIEW_CONFIGS. Missing configs cause KeyError at runtime. 43→**adding a subprocess call to the TUI without stderr inspection** → Read [TUI Subprocess Feedback Patterns](subprocess-feedback.md) first. TUI subprocess calls should inspect stderr for known success/failure markers. See subprocess-feedback.md for the pattern. 63→**calling start_operation() without a matching finish_operation() in the error path** → Read [TUI Multi-Operation Tracking](multi-operation-tracking.md) first. Always call finish_operation() in both success and error paths. Use try/finally or explicit error handling. Missing finish calls leave ghost operations in the status bar. 73→**constructing PlanFilters without copying all fields from existing filters** → Read [TUI Data Contract](data-contract.md) first. All fields must be explicitly copied in \_load_data() PlanFilters construction. Missing fields (like creator) cause silent filtering failures. 87→**extending PlanDataProvider ABC** → Read [TUI Architecture Overview](architecture.md) first. Requires 3-file update: abc.py + real.py + fake.py. Fake must initialize new dict in **init**. Missing init causes AttributeError at test time. 115→**removing a field from a frozen dataclass** → Read [Frozen Dataclass Field Management](frozen-dataclass-field-management.md) first. Grep for the class name across ALL constructor sites. Frozen dataclasses have 5+ places to update: field definition, real provider, fake provider, test helpers, and filtering/display logic. Missing one causes runtime TypeError.

1→"""View mode types for TUI dashboard.""" 2→ 3→from __future__ import annotations 4→ 5→from dataclasses import dataclass 6→from enum import Enum, auto 7→ 8→ 9→class ViewMode(Enum): 10→ """Available view modes for the dashboard.""" 11→ 12→ PLANS = auto() 13→ LEARN = auto() 14→ OBJECTIVES = auto() 15→ 16→ 17→@dataclass(frozen=True) 18→class ViewConfig: 19→ """Configuration for a specific view mode. 20→ 21→ Attributes: 22→ mode: The view mode this config describes 23→ display_name: Human-readable name for the view 24→ labels: GitHub labels to use when fetching data 25→ key_hint: Key binding hint (e.g., "1", "2", "3") 26→ exclude_labels: Labels to exclude from results (client-side filtering 27→ applied before expensive enrichment). Empty tuple means no exclusion. 28→ """ 29→ 30→ mode: ViewMode

1→"""Data types for TUI components.""" 2→ 3→from __future__ import annotations 4→ 5→from dataclasses import dataclass 6→from datetime import datetime 7→ 8→from erk_shared.gateway.github.types import IssueFilterState 9→ 10→ 11→@dataclass(frozen=True) 12→class PlanRowData: 13→ """Row data for displaying a plan in the TUI table. 14→ 15→ Contains pre-formatted display strings and raw data needed for actions. 16→ Immutable to ensure table state consistency. 17→ 18→ **Display vs Raw convention:** Many data points have both a raw field (for 19→ logic/predicates) and a display field (for table rendering). Raw fields are 20→ often nullable (None when absent), while display fields are always strings 21→ (dash "-" or empty when absent). Use raw fields in availability predicates 22→ (``ctx.row.pr_number is not None``), display fields for rendering. 23→ 24→ Attributes: 25→ 26→ plan_id: GitHub issue number (e.g., 123). Never None. 27→ plan_url: Full URL to the GitHub issue. None when unavailable. 28→ full_title: Complete untruncated plan title. Empty string possible. 29→ plan_body: Raw issue body text (markdown). Empty string possible. 30→

200→ start_time = time.monotonic() 201→ 202→ # Snapshot view mode at fetch start to avoid race conditions. 203→ # If the user switches tabs during the fetch, self._view_mode changes 204→ # but fetched_mode retains the original value so results are cached 205→ # under the correct key and stale results don't overwrite the display. 206→ fetched_mode = self._view_mode 207→ view_config = get_view_config(fetched_mode) 208→ active_creator = None if self._show_all_users else self._original_creator 209→ active_filters = PlanFilters( 210→ labels=view_config.labels, 211→ state=self._plan_filters.state, 212→ run_state=self._plan_filters.run_state, 213→ limit=self._plan_filters.limit, 214→ show_prs=self._plan_filters.show_prs, 215→ show_runs=self._plan_filters.show_runs, 216→ creator=active_creator, 217→ exclude_labels=view_config.exclude_labels, 218→ ) 219→ 220→ fetch_timings: FetchTimings | None = None 221→ try: 222→ # Run sync fetch in executor to avoid blocking 223→ loop = asyncio.get_running_loop() 224→ rows, fetch_timings = await loop.run_in_executor( 225→ None, self._provider.fetch_plans, active_filters 226→ ) 227→ 228→ # If sorting by activity, also fetch activity data 229→ if self._sort_state.key == SortKey.BRANCH_ACTIVITY:

1→"""Shared utilities for run commands.""" 2→ 3→import re 4→ 5→ 6→def extract_pr_number(display_title: str | None) -> int | None: 7→ """Extract PR number from display_title containing '#NNN'. 8→ 9→ Handles: 10→ - "pr-address:#456:abc123" → 456 11→ - "8559:#460:abc123" → 460 12→ - "one-shot:#458:abc123" → 458 13→ - "rebase:#456:abc123" → 456 14→ - "8559:abc123" (old format, no #) → None 15→ - "Some title [abc123]" (legacy) → None 16→ - None or empty → None 17→ """ 18→ if not display_title: 19→ return None 20→ match = re.search(r"#(\d+)", display_title) 21→ if match is None: 22→ return None 23→ return int(match.group(1)) 24→ 25→ 26→def extract_plan_number(display_title: str | None) -> int | None: 27→ """Extract plan number from display_title format '123:abc456'. 28→ 29→ Handles: 30→ - Legacy format: "123:abc456" → 123 (plan_id at start, no #pr_number)
... [19 lines omitted] ...

1→"""Display formatting utilities for erk. 2→ 3→This module contains pure business logic for formatting and displaying worktree 4→information in the CLI. All functions are pure (no I/O) and can be tested without 5→filesystem access. 6→""" 7→ 8→import base64 9→import re 10→from datetime import datetime 11→ 12→import click 13→ 14→from erk_shared.gateway.github.types import PullRequestInfo, WorkflowRun 15→ 16→ 17→def get_visible_length(text: str) -> int: 18→ """Calculate the visible length of text, excluding ANSI and OSC escape sequences. 19→ 20→ Args: 21→ text: Text that may contain escape sequences 22→ 23→ Returns: 24→ Number of visible characters 25→ """ 26→ # Remove ANSI color codes (\033[...m) 27→ text = re.sub(r"\033\[[0-9;]*m", "", text) 28→ # Remove OSC 8 hyperlink sequences (\033]8;;URL\033\\) 29→ text = re.sub(r"\033\]8;;[^\033]*\033\\", "", text) 30→ return len(text)

1→"""Shared constants for erk CLI commands.""" 2→ 3→# GitHub issue label for erk plans 4→ERK_PLAN_LABEL = "erk-plan" 5→ 6→# Title prefix for erk-plan issues (with trailing space for easy stripping) 7→ERK_PLAN_TITLE_PREFIX = "[erk-plan] " 8→ 9→# Plan markdown heading prefix (with trailing space for easy stripping) 10→PLAN_HEADING_PREFIX = "Plan: " 11→ 12→# PR title prefix for plan-originated PRs 13→PLANNED_PR_TITLE_PREFIX = "plnd/" 14→ 15→# GitHub Actions workflow for remote implementation dispatch 16→DISPATCH_WORKFLOW_NAME = "plan-implement.yml" 17→DISPATCH_WORKFLOW_METADATA_NAME = "plan-implement" 18→ 19→# GitHub Actions workflow for remote rebase with conflict resolution 20→REBASE_WORKFLOW_NAME = "pr-rebase.yml" 21→ 22→# GitHub Actions workflow for remote PR comment addressing 23→PR_ADDRESS_WORKFLOW_NAME = "pr-address.yml" 24→ 25→# GitHub Actions workflow for remote PR rewrite (rebase + AI PR summary) 26→PR_REWRITE_WORKFLOW_NAME = "pr-rewrite.yml" 27→ 28→# GitHub Actions workflow for one-shot autonomous execution 29→ONE_SHOT_WORKFLOW_NAME = "one-shot.yml" 30→

456→def format_submission_time(created_at: datetime | None) -> str: 457→ """Format workflow run submission time as MM-DD HH:MM in local timezone. 458→ 459→ Args: 460→ created_at: UTC datetime when run was created, or None 461→ 462→ Returns: 463→ Formatted string like "11-26 14:30" in local timezone, or "[dim]-[/dim]" if None 464→ """ 465→ if created_at is None: 466→ return "[dim]-[/dim]" 467→ 468→ # Convert UTC to local timezone 469→ local_time = created_at.astimezone() 470→ 471→ # Format as MM-DD HH:MM 472→ return local_time.strftime("%m-%d %H:%M") 473→ 474→ 475→def format_relative_time(iso_timestamp: str | None, now: datetime | None = None) -> str: 476→ """Format ISO timestamp as human-readable relative time. 477→ 478→ Args: 479→ iso_timestamp: ISO 8601 timestamp string, or None 480→ now: Optional current time for testing (defaults to datetime.now(UTC)) 481→ 482→ Returns: 483→ Relative time string like "just now", "5m ago", "2h ago", "3d ago" 484→ Returns empty string if iso_timestamp is None or invalid 485→ """

session.assistant:
Perfect! Now I have all the information needed. Let me create a comprehensive summary document of the TUI codebase structure for adding the Runs tab:
## Comprehensive TUI Codebase Structure for Adding "Runs" Tab
Based on my exploration of the erk TUI codebase, here's a complete reference document for implementing the new "Runs" tab feature:
---
### 1. VIEW MODE AND CONFIGURATION (src/erk/tui/views/types.py)
The ViewMode enum currently has 3 modes: ```python class ViewMode(Enum): PLANS = auto() LEARN = auto() OBJECTIVES = auto() ```
To add a RUNS view, you need to: - Add `RUNS = auto()` to ViewMode enum - Create a `RUNS_VIEW` ViewConfig with display_name, labels, key_hint, exclude_labels - Add RUNS_VIEW to VIEW_CONFIGS tuple - Update `_VIEW_CONFIG_BY_MODE` dictionary (auto-populated from VIEW_CONFIGS) - The structure includes: - `mode`: ViewMode enum value - `display_name`: Human-readable name (e.g., "Runs") - `labels`: GitHub labels tuple (e.g., `()` for fetching runs directly) - `key_hint`: Key binding hint (e.g., "4" for 4th tab) - `exclude_labels`: Labels to exclude from results
The `get_next_view_mode()` and `get_previous_view_mode()` functions automatically cycle through VIEW_CONFIGS.
---
### 2. RUN ROW DATA STRUCTURE (src/erk/tui/data/types.py)
Currently defines `PlanRowData` frozen dataclass with ~50 fields. For a Runs view, you'd create a similar immutable dataclass with run-specific fields:
Key pattern to follow: - Raw data fields (nullable): `run_id`, `run_status`, `workflow_run`, etc. - Display fields (always strings): `run_id_display`, `run_state_display`, etc. - Use pre-formatted strings at fetch time, not at render time - All fields should be frozen dataclass attributes
Fields from the existing CLI list_runs command that map to runs: - run_id (string ID from GitHub) - workflow_name (from WORKFLOW_COMMAND_MAP) - pr_number (extracted from run display_title) - plan_number (extracted from run display_title via extract_plan_number) - created_at (timestamp) - status (queued, in_progress, completed) - conclusion (success, failure, cancelled, None) - run_url (computed from location + run_id)
---
### 3. DATA PROVIDER ABC (src/erk/tui/data/provider_abc.py)
The `PlanDataProvider` ABC defines the interface: ```python class PlanDataProvider(ABC): @abstractmethod def fetch_plans(self, filters: PlanFilters) -> tuple[list[PlanRowData], FetchTimings | None]: ...
@abstractmethod def fetch_plans_by_ids(self, plan_ids: set[int]) -> list[PlanRowData]: ...
@abstractmethod def fetch_plans_for_objective(self, objective_issue: int) -> list[PlanRowData]: ...
@abstractmethod def fetch_branch_activity(self, rows: list[PlanRowData]) -> dict[int, BranchActivity]: ... ```
For a separate Runs view, you could: - **Option A**: Extend PlanDataProvider with new methods like `fetch_runs()` - **Option B**: Create a parallel `RunDataProvider` ABC with its own interface - **Option C**: Keep both as single provider but route based on ViewMode
The pattern shows: - Abstract methods return typed tuples with optional FetchTimings - Methods handle filtering and transformation to display rows - Fakes return canned data without API calls
---
### 4. REAL DATA PROVIDER (src/erk/tui/data/real_provider.py)
`RealPlanDataProvider` handles the transformation from gateway data to display rows. Key method structure:
```python def fetch_plans(self, filters: PlanFilters) -> tuple[list[PlanRowData], FetchTimings | None]: # 1. Route to appropriate service (objective vs plan) # 2. Fetch worktree mapping # 3. Transform each plan to PlanRowData via _build_row_data() # 4. Return with FetchTimings breakdown ```
The `_build_row_data()` pattern: - Accepts individual plan + related data (PR linkages, workflow run, etc.) - Extracts fields from headers - Formats display strings using utility functions - Returns fully constructed PlanRowData
For Runs, the equivalent would: - Fetch runs from `ctx.github.list_workflow_runs()` - Extract PR/plan numbers from display_title via `extract_pr_number()`, `extract_plan_number()` - Build location from first run URL - Format run_id, status, submission_time using display utilities - Return RunRowData with all display fields pre-formatted
Key formatting helpers already exist: - `format_workflow_run_id(workflow_run, url)` → formatted with link - `format_workflow_outcome(workflow_run)` → emoji + status - `format_submission_time(created_at)` → MM-DD HH:MM local time - `format_relative_time(iso_timestamp)` → "2h ago" style
---
### 5. FAKE DATA PROVIDER (src/erk/tui/data/fake_provider.py & packages/erk-shared/src/erk_shared/gateway/plan_data_provider/fake.py)
Two versions exist (one in tui, one in shared). Both: - Take optional `plans` list in __init__ - Support `plans_by_labels` for view-specific data - Track `fetch_count` property - Support `set_plans()` to update test data - Implement all ABC methods returning canned data
The `make_plan_row()` helper in fake.py: - Factory function with sensible defaults - Accepts keyword args for all PlanRowData fields - Computes complex display fields from raw values - Used in all TUI tests for consistent test data
For RunRowData, you'd create parallel: - `FakeRunDataProvider` class - `make_run_row()` helper function
---
### 6. PLAN DATA TABLE WIDGET (src/erk/tui/widgets/plan_table.py)
The `PlanDataTable` DataTable subclass shows the pattern:
Key responsibilities: - Column setup in `_setup_columns()` based on ViewMode - Row-to-values conversion in `_row_to_values(row)` matching column order - Click detection in `on_click()` with custom Message posts - Cursor position preservation in `populate()` by row key
Column setup pattern: ```python def _setup_columns(self) -> None: col_index = 0
# ViewMode-specific columns if self._view_mode == ViewMode.OBJECTIVES: # One set of columns else: # Different set
# Store indices for click detection: self._plan_column_index, etc. self.add_column(header, key="binding_name", width=N) ```
The `add_column()` contract: - `key=` parameter must match a field name in row data (for DataTable binding) - All values must be provided in `_row_to_values()` in exact column order - Rich markup supported in Text() objects
For RunDataTable: - Create separate widget (or reuse with ViewMode routing) - Define columns: run-id, status, submitted, workflow, pr, title, chks - Implement _row_to_values() producing strings/Text in order - Add click handlers for run-id, pr columns
---
### 7. MAIN APP (src/erk/tui/app.py)
`ErkDashApp` orchestrates everything:
Key initialization: ```python def __init__( self, *, provider: PlanDataProvider, service: PlanService, filters: PlanFilters, refresh_interval: float = 15.0, initial_sort: SortState | None = None, cmux_integration: bool = False, ) -> None: ```
Key methods: - `compose()` → creates layout (Header, ViewBar, PlanDataTable, StatusBar) - `on_mount()` → initializes and starts `_load_data()` worker - `_load_data()` → async fetch in executor, caches by view labels - `_switch_view(mode)` → handles tab switching, clears filters, reconfigures table - `_apply_filter_and_sort()` → applies text/stack/objective filters - Event handlers (@on decorated) for clicks, input changes, view switching
View switching pattern: ```python def _switch_view(self, mode: ViewMode) -> None: if mode == self._view_mode: return
# Clear filters self._stack_filter_branches = None self._objective_filter_issue = None
# Reconfigure table self._table.reconfigure(plan_filters=self._plan_filters, view_mode=mode)
# Check cache or fetch fresh cached = self._data_cache.get(view_config.labels) if cached: self._rows = self._apply_filter_and_sort(cached) self._table.populate(self._rows) else: self.run_worker(self._load_data(), exclusive=True) ```
Bindings include view switches: - `Binding("1", "switch_view_plans", ...)` - `Binding("2", "switch_view_learn", ...)` - `Binding("3", "switch_view_objectives", ...)`
For a 4th Runs tab: - Add `Binding("4", "action_switch_view_runs", ...)` - Implement `action_switch_view_runs()` method - Add "4" to key hints in HelpScreen
---
### 8. HELP SCREEN (src/erk/tui/screens/help_screen.py)
Modal screen showing keyboard shortcuts. View-dependent content:
```python def __init__(self, *, view_mode: ViewMode) -> None: self._view_mode = view_mode
def _is_objectives_view(self) -> bool: return self._view_mode == ViewMode.OBJECTIVES
def compose(self) -> ComposeResult: is_objectives = self._is_objectives_view()
# Different content based on view mode if is_objectives: yield Label("Enter View objective details", ...) else: yield Label("Enter View plan details", ...) ```
To add Runs tab help: - Add `_is_runs_view()` method - Add new conditional block in compose() for Runs-specific shortcuts - Document run-specific actions (open run, open PR from run, etc.)
---
### 9. RUN LIST COMMAND PATTERN (src/erk/cli/commands/run/list_cmd.py)
Existing `_list_runs()` shows the implementation pattern to port:
```python def _list_runs(ctx: ErkContext) -> None: # 1. Fetch workflow runs from all registered workflows tagged_runs: list[tuple[WorkflowRun, str]] = [] for command_name, workflow_file in WORKFLOW_COMMAND_MAP.items(): workflow_runs = ctx.github.list_workflow_runs(repo.root, workflow_file, _PER_WORKFLOW_LIMIT) for run in workflow_runs: tagged_runs.append((run, command_name))
# 2. Deduplicate by run_id seen: dict[str, tuple[WorkflowRun, str]] = {} for pair in tagged_runs: if pair[0].run_id not in seen: seen[pair[0].run_id] = pair
# 3. Sort by created_at descending tagged_runs.sort( key=lambda pair: pair[0].created_at or datetime.min.replace(tzinfo=UTC), reverse=True, )
# 4. Limit displayed runs tagged_runs = tagged_runs[:_MAX_DISPLAY_RUNS]
# 5. Extract PR/plan numbers from display_titles run_pr_numbers: dict[str, int] = {} plan_numbers: list[int] = []
for run, _workflow_name in tagged_runs: pr_num = extract_pr_number(run.display_title) if pr_num: run_pr_numbers[run.run_id] = pr_num else: plan_num = extract_plan_number(run.display_title) if plan_num: plan_numbers.append(plan_num)
# 6. Build table with run-id, status, submitted, workflow, pr, title, chks ```
Key functions to use: - `ctx.github.list_workflow_runs(repo_root, workflow_file, limit)` - `extract_pr_number(display_title)` from run/shared.py - `extract_plan_number(display_title)` from run/shared.py - `WORKFLOW_COMMAND_MAP` from constants.py
---
### 10. SHARED UTILITIES
**extract_pr_number() and extract_plan_number() (src/erk/cli/commands/run/shared.py)**: ```python def extract_pr_number(display_title: str | None) -> int | None: # Looks for "#NNN" pattern in display_title # Returns int(match.group(1)) or None
def extract_plan_number(display_title: str | None) -> int | None: # Looks for "NNN:..." pattern (leading digits before colon) # Returns int(first_part) or None if not found/not digit ```
**Display utilities (src/erk/core/display_utils.py)**: - `format_workflow_run_id(workflow_run, workflow_url)` → Rich markup with link - `format_workflow_outcome(workflow_run)` → emoji + status text - `format_submission_time(created_at)` → "MM-DD HH:MM" local time - `format_relative_time(iso_timestamp)` → "2h ago" style - `get_workflow_run_state(workflow_run)` → normalized state string
**Constants (src/erk/cli/constants.py)**: ```python WORKFLOW_COMMAND_MAP: dict[str, str] = { "plan-implement": "plan-implement.yml", "pr-rebase": "pr-rebase.yml", "pr-address": "pr-address.yml", "pr-rewrite": "pr-rewrite.yml", "learn": "learn.yml", "one-shot": "one-shot.yml", } ```
---
### KEY INTEGRATION POINTS
1. **ViewMode enum** → triggers table reconfiguration 2. **PlanFilters/RunFilters** → controls what data is fetched 3. **Provider interface** → abstracts data fetching for testability 4. **FetchTimings** → performance monitoring (rest_issues_ms, workflow_runs_ms, etc.) 5. **PlanDataTable.populate()** → cursor preservation by key (str(plan_id)) 6. **_apply_filter_and_sort()** → filter pipeline order matters (objective → stack → text → sort) 7. **Caching by view labels** → `_data_cache[view_config.labels]` prevents data loss on tab switch 8. **Worker threads** → all I/O in `run_worker()` to avoid blocking UI
---
### CRITICAL PATTERNS FROM TRIPWIRES
1. **Column addition**: Must update 5 places: field definition, make_plan_row(), _setup_columns(), _row_to_values(), and test helpers 2. **Frozen dataclasses**: Always use keyword arguments for construction, never positional 3. **ViewMode without VIEW_CONFIGS**: Causes KeyError at runtime 4. **Missing view predicates on commands**: Commands appear in all views instead of filtered 5. **Async state snapshot**: Snapshot view_mode at fetch start (`fetched_mode = self._view_mode`), not during fetch 6. **Filter order**: objective → stack → text → sort (changing order breaks filtering) 7. **Display strings**: Pre-format during provider, use at render time (not during render) 8. **LocalWorktreeNotFound**: All PlanRowData must exist even if some fields are None/empty
This completes the structural reference for implementing the new "Runs" tab in the TUI dashboard.
